headline: Samsung, SK Hynix tumble 9%+ as chip rout spreads
detail: A 9%+ drop in the world's top memory chipmakers signals serious sector-wide fear, not just individual stock weakness. This directly pressures SMH, SOXL, and any semi-adjacent name in your book. On margin and in leveraged ETFs, the knock-on effect arrives faster and harder.
rec: Watch SOXL ($200 stop) and SPAL ($21.38 stop) closely today - these are your most exposed positions to this exact headline. No new semi buys while this rout is live.

headline: Amazon designing its own AI chips for Echo and Fire TV
detail: Amazon building in-house chips is a long-term headwind for merchant silicon vendors and could pressure sentiment around chip names broadly. Short term it adds another reason the market is questioning semi demand outlooks today.
rec: No immediate trade - this is a slow-burn story, but it adds to today's negative semi sentiment. Worth monitoring how TSM and ARM react since both appear in your scanner watchlist.

headline: BYD and Xiaomi surge on strong June delivery figures
detail: Strong EV delivery numbers out of China lift sentiment in that specific pocket but have minimal direct read-through to your tech/chip-heavy book. Indirectly, China tech strength could support some AI infrastructure demand narratives longer term.
rec: No action for your portfolio today - this headline doesn't move your specific names and doesn't pair with any scanner setup worth acting on now.

headline: Oil falls as Middle East tensions ease - worst quarter for Brent since 2020
detail: Lower oil is generally a mild positive for tech multiples (lower input costs, less inflation pressure) but the chip sector rout is dominating today and overshadows any macro tailwind from energy. Not a catalyst to buy your names into a falling tape.
rec: No action - the macro tailwind from oil is real but too small to offset the active semi selloff hitting your book right now.
